seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with Instructor enables reliable structured extraction from conversational LLMs, ensuring responses adhere to Pydantic schemas for intent classification and RAG retrieval. However, conversational agents face high risks like hallucinated enum values, inconsistent nested extractions, and coherence failures across multi-turn interactions. Vex guardrails are essential to verify response accuracy, safety, and PII protection before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Chatbot follows a RAG pipeline: user input triggers Instructor-wrapped OpenAI calls for intent classification (e.g., 'query', 'greeting') and entity extraction. Retrieved documents from vector DB augment the prompt, with Instructor ensuring structured tool responses. Multi-turn memory maintains conversation state via session-scoped Pydantic models, validated at each turn for coherence.

Instructor patches the OpenAI client to retry failed validations automatically, but exhaustion occurs with complex nested schemas or adversarial inputs. Vex adds runtime verification for safety, PII redaction, and hallucination detection beyond schema enforcement.

The architecture scales with async processing for real-time chat, using Redis for memory and Pinecone for retrieval, with all critical paths wrapped in Vex for production reliability.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐
│   User Input    │───▶│ Instructor Intent │
│     (Chat)      │    │   Classification  │
└─────────────────┘    │     (Pydantic)    │
                       └────────┬──────────┘
                                │
                       ┌────────▼──────────┐
                       │ Vector DB (RAG)   │
                       │   Retrieval       │
                       └────────┬──────────┘
                                │
                       ┌────────▼──────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐
                       │ Vex Verification  │───▶│ Structured      │
                       │ (Safety/PII/Vex)  │    │   Response      │
                       └──────────────────┘    └─────────────────┘

Multi-turn: Redis Memory ──→ Context Injection ──→ Instructor Patch

risks.name: Structured output validation retries exhausted
risks.description: Instructor retries fail after max_attempts when model consistently violates complex nested schemas in multi-turn extraction
risks.mitigation: Vex fallback routing to safer models + async verification before retry exhaustion

risks.name: Model hallucinating enum values outside schema
risks.description: LLM generates invalid intent enums like 'unknown_query' instead of schema-defined 'query|greeting|help'
risks.mitigation: Vex enum validation + hallucination detection scoring enum predictions

risks.name: Partial extraction missing required fields
risks.description: RAG responses omit critical fields like 'retrieved_docs' or 'confidence_score' from nested Response model
risks.mitigation: Vex required field enforcement + completeness scoring

risks.name: Safety/PII leakage in conversational memory
risks.description: Multi-turn context accumulates unredacted PII across sessions despite Instructor extraction
risks.mitigation: Vex PII scanning + automatic redaction before memory persistence

implementation.title: Intent Classification with Instructor
implementation.description: Patch OpenAI client and extract structured intent from user messages
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from openai import OpenAI
from typing import Literal

class Intent(BaseModel):
    intent: Literal['query', 'greeting', 'help', 'goodbye'] = Field(..., description='User intent')
    entities: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    confidence: float

client = from_openai(OpenAI())

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=Intent,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What's the weather like?"}]
)
print(response.intent)  # 'query'

implementation.title: RAG Response Extraction
implementation.description: Extract structured response with retrieved context validation
implementation.code:
class ChatResponse(BaseModel):
    answer: str
    retrieved_docs: list[str]
    confidence: float
    sources: list[str]

# After retrieval
rag_context = ["doc1", "doc2"]
response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o',
    response_model=ChatResponse,
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": f"Context: {rag_context}"},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Summarize this"}
    ]
)
print(f"Answer: {response.answer}")

implementation.title: TypeScript Instructor Client Patching
implementation.description: Instructor.js equivalent for Node.js chatbots with Zod schemas

implementation.title: Multi-turn Memory Management
implementation.description: Maintain conversation state with validated session models
implementation.code:
class ConversationState(BaseModel):
    session_id: str
    history: list[dict]
    user_context: dict
    last_intent: str

# Redis-backed memory
state = ConversationState(
    session_id="chat_123",
    history=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}],
    user_context={},
    last_intent="greeting"
)

# Validate before persisting
validated_state = client.chat.completions.create(
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    response_model=ConversationState,
    messages=[{"role": "system", "content": state.model_dump_json()}]
)

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Instructor with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex runtime checks for accuracy, safety, and PII around Instructor calls
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from instructor import from_openai

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

@pvex_client.verify(
    checks=[
        "response_accuracy",
        "conversation_coherence",
        "safety_filtering",
        "pii_protection",
        "hallucination_detection"
    ]
)
def safe_chat_completion(messages: list, response_model):
    client = from_openai(OpenAI())
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model='gpt-4o',
        messages=messages,
        response_model=response_model
    )

# Usage
result = safe_chat_completion(
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "My SSN is 123-45-6789"}],
    response_model=Intent
)
# Vex auto-redacts PII, validates coherence
